I’m continuing by locating the exact website diagram component and the current BIQc messaging in the codebase, then I’ll draft a concrete update plan that makes the value proposition clear within 30 seconds.
I found the section in `HomePage.js`; next I’m reading the diagram component and adjacent website messaging so the plan aligns with BIQc’s current product flow, not just visual polish.

I’ve confirmed the current homepage uses a legacy-style flow diagram, while there’s already richer, newer BIQc capability copy elsewhere. I’m now checking those source pages so the update plan is grounded in current product truth.
I’ve identified two strong implementation paths (lightweight refresh vs. full homepage “how it works” restructure), so I need one quick direction choice before finalizing the plan.
Perfect—based on your choices, I’m finalizing a plan to promote the richer `HowItWorks` module on the homepage and tune it for a fast decision-making message.

<attached_files>

<code_selection path="file:///c%3A/Users/andre/.cursor/plans/homepage-biqc-flow-refresh_b447d366.plan.md" lines="1-60">
L1:---
L2:name: homepage-biqc-flow-refresh
L3:overview: Replace the outdated homepage diagram with the richer BIQc "How It Works" module, then tune the first 30 seconds of copy and structure to emphasize faster leadership decisions.
L4:todos:
L5:  - id: swap-home-module
L6:    content: Replace legacy `IntelligenceDiagram` usage with `HowItWorks` on homepage and adjust adjacent section copy.
L7:    status: pending
L8:  - id: optimize-30s-message
L9:    content: Add/adjust a top-level 30-second summary strip in `HowItWorks` with faster-decision messaging.
L10:    status: pending
L11:  - id: mobile-alignment
L12:    content: Update `mobile.css` selectors/styles to match promoted module and remove dependence on old diagram IDs.
L13:    status: pending
L14:  - id: qa-pass
L15:    content: Run targeted UI sanity checks for homepage + mobile responsiveness + stable test IDs.
L16:    status: pending
L17:isProject: false
L18:---
L19:
L20:# Refresh Homepage BIQc Flow Module
L21:
L22:## Objective
L23:Make BIQc understandable in under 30 seconds for new visitors by replacing the legacy static diagram with the richer current-state flow and decision-centric messaging.
L24:
L25:## Current-state anchors
L26:- Homepage currently mounts the outdated visual block via `[frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js](frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js)` using `"<IntelligenceDiagram />"`.
L27:- Current product-truth content already exists in `[frontend/src/components/website/HowItWorks.js](frontend/src/components/website/HowItWorks.js)` with verified structures: `SIX_LAYERS`, `SIX_CATEGORIES`, and `PIPELINE_STEPS`.
L28:- Mobile overrides still target legacy selectors in `[frontend/src/mobile.css](frontend/src/mobile.css)` under `[data-testid="intelligence-diagram"]` and `[data-testid="biqc-core-node"]`.
L29:
L30:## Proposed implementation
L31:- Replace the homepage diagram insertion in `[frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js](frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js)`:
L32:  - Remove `IntelligenceDiagram` import/usage.
L33:  - Render `HowItWorks` in the same page position.
L34:  - Update/condense nearby heading copy so the section opens with decision-speed language.
L35:- Upgrade `[frontend/src/components/website/HowItWorks.js](frontend/src/components/website/HowItWorks.js)` for 30-second comprehension:
L36:  - Add a compact top "30-second executive scan" row (4 tiles: Connect, Monitor, Detect, Decide/Act).
L37:  - Reword hero line/subline to emphasize faster leadership decisions (your selected priority).
L38:  - Keep deeper tabs/layers for visitors who continue reading.
L39:- Align responsive behavior in `[frontend/src/mobile.css](frontend/src/mobile.css)`:
L40:  - Retire old diagram-specific rules or remap them to the new `how-it-works` test IDs.
L41:  - Ensure mobile readability for labels/cards in the promoted module.
L42:- Preserve tracking/test stability:
L43:  - Keep or add stable `data-testid` markers on the new homepage section so existing QA flows remain reliable.
L44:
L45:## Information architecture (visitor understanding in <30s)
L46:```mermaid
L47:flowchart TD
L48:  visitor[VisitorLands] -->|"sees in 30 seconds"| connect[ConnectSystems]
L49:  connect --> monitor[MonitorSignals]
L50:  monitor --> detect[DetectRisksAndOpportunities]
L51:  detect --> act[DecideAndActFaster]
L52:  act --> outcome[ClearLeadershipOutcomes]
L53:```
L54:
L55:## Validation checklist
L56:- Homepage loads with promoted `HowItWorks` content and no regressions in surrounding sections.
L57:- First viewport-to-module transition communicates BIQc value in one fast narrative.
L58:- Mobile layout remains readable and uncluttered.
L59:- No broken imports, console errors, or visual overflow in website routes.
L60:
</code_selection>

</attached_files>
<user_query>
Refresh Homepage BIQc Flow Module

Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself.

To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.
</user_query>

Implementing now. I’ll start with the first todo (`swap-home-module`) by updating the homepage imports/section wiring, then proceed through the remaining todos in order and run a quick verification pass at the end.
I’m editing `HomePage.js` now to swap in the richer `HowItWorks` module and tighten the introductory copy for faster decision-speed positioning.
Next I’m enhancing `HowItWorks.js` with a compact 30-second executive scan row and revising the top messaging to emphasize faster leadership decisions.
I’m now updating `mobile.css` to remove legacy `intelligence-diagram` targeting and apply equivalent mobile behavior to the promoted `HowItWorks` module, including the new 30-second scan row.
I’m running a frontend build now to validate imports/JSX/mobile CSS changes compile cleanly and to catch any regressions before I close out the last todo.
I spotted one small reliability improvement before wrapping: I’m making the 30-second step test IDs explicit (`connect`, `monitor`, `detect`, `decide-act`) so selectors stay stable and clean.

Implemented the full **Refresh Homepage BIQc Flow Module** plan (without touching the plan file), and completed all four todos. ### What changed - Replaced the homepage’s legacy diagram mount with the richer `HowItWorks` module and updated the section intro copy to a faster-decision message in `frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js`. - Updated the top of `HowItWorks` for 30-second clarity in `frontend/src/components/website/HowItWorks.js`: - New decision-focused header/subheader copy. - Added a compact 4-step “30-second executive scan” row (`Connect`, `Monitor`, `Detect`, `Decide & Act`). - Added stable test IDs for each scan tile. - Remapped old mobile overrides from legacy diagram selectors to the promoted module in `frontend/src/mobile.css` (`[data-testid="how-it-works"]`). ```141:158:frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js {/* How it all works block under hero */}

How BIQc works

Connect your systems once. BIQc monitors live signals, detects what matters, and gives leadership clear decisions faster.

``` ```186:222:frontend/src/components/website/HowItWorks.js
How It Works

From disconnected systems to  faster leadership decisions.

In under 30 seconds: connect your tools, monitor live signals, detect issues early, and act with confidence.

{EXECUTIVE_SCAN_STEPS.map((step) => (
``` ```121:131:frontend/src/mobile.css /* ── Website How It Works module ── */ [data-testid="how-it-works"] { padding-top: 32px !important; padding-bottom: 32px !important; } [data-testid="how-it-works"] .sm\:grid-cols-3 { grid-template-columns: 1fr !important; } [data-testid="how-it-works"] .lg\:grid-cols-4 { grid-template-columns: 1fr !important; } ``` ### Validation run - Ran static checks via search to confirm: - `HomePage` now uses `HowItWorks`. - new 30-second test IDs exist. - mobile CSS no longer targets `intelligence-diagram`/`biqc-core-node`. - Attempted build with `npm run build` in `frontend`, but environment lacks local deps (`craco` not found), so compile validation is currently blocked until install. ### Suggested next command - In `frontend`: `npm install` then `npm run build` to complete runtime/compile verification.

Displayed in terminal. I ran:

`git diff -- frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js frontend/src/components/website/HowItWorks.js frontend/src/mobile.css`

Key terminal highlights:
- `HomePage.js`: swapped legacy diagram usage for `HowItWorks`, updated the “How BIQc works” intro copy to a faster-decision message.
- `HowItWorks.js`: added `EXECUTIVE_SCAN_STEPS` (Connect, Monitor, Detect, Decide & Act) plus the new 30-second scan row with stable `data-testid`s.
- `mobile.css`: replaced old `intelligence-diagram` selectors with `how-it-works` responsive rules.

If you want, I can also show a compact terminal view with just changed hunk headers (`git diff --stat` + `git diff -U3`).
